response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts and bots. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Identify suspicious profiles: Be cautious of profiles that have a high number of followers or likes but no original content. Check their profile picture, bio, and posts for any inconsistencies. 2) Use fact-checking tools: Utilize third-party fact-checking tools to verify the accuracy of information being shared on social media. 3) Report fake accounts: If you encounter a fake account, report it to the platform immediately. Most platforms have policies against spreading false information and will take action against such accounts. 4) Follow reputable sources: Stick to verified sources of information and news to avoid falling for misinformation. 5) Be cautious of sensational headlines: Be wary of sensational headlines that seem too good (or bad) to be true. Verify the information before sharing or believing it. By taking these steps, you can better manage and deal with fake content on social media."
,
"reasoning_summary": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts and bots. This includes identifying suspicious profiles, using fact-checking tools, reporting fake accounts, following reputable sources, and being cautious of sensational headlines.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts and bots. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Identify suspicious profiles: Be cautious of profiles that have a high number of followers or likes but no original content. Check their profile picture, bio, and posts for any inconsistencies. 2) Use fact-checking tools: Utilize third-party fact-checking tools to verify the accuracy of information being shared on social media. 3) Report fake accounts: If you encounter a fake account, report it to the platform immediately. Most platforms have policies against spreading false information and will take action against such accounts. 4) Follow reputable sources: Stick to verified sources of information and news to avoid falling for misinformation. 5) Be cautious of sensational headlines: Be wary of sensational headlines that seem too good (or bad) to be true. Verify the information before sharing or believing it. By taking these steps, you can better manage and deal with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts and bots. This includes identifying suspicious profiles, using fact-checking tools, reporting fake accounts, following reputable sources, and being cautious of sensational headlines.
